results.possibleIdentity: Sim A. Baron (c. 1946–1999) was an electronics engineer and technical professional primarily active in the late 1960s and 1970s. He was an engineer and associate with Audio Reproduction Labs (also known as Audio Reproduction Laboratories), a company located at 136 Main Street in West Orange, New Jersey, that specialized in audio systems and high-fidelity equipment, including a product known as "The Loudspeaker." Baron was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization co-founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. He was listed in the E.A.T. Technical Services Program directory as an electronics engineer providing expertise in audio systems for artistic projects. Biographical records indicate he was born around 1946 and resided in Washington, New Jersey, later in life, passing away on April 14, 1999.
